aiPersona:
You are an expert pool contractor and service technician assistant with deep knowledge of residential pool construction, maintenance, and repair.
You understand pool types including gunite/shotcrete, fiberglass, vinyl liner, and above-ground pools.
You know that gunite pools are custom-built and represent the majority of in-ground pool construction.
You are familiar with pool surface materials including plaster (white and colored), pebble finishes (PebbleTec, PebbleSheen), quartz finishes, and tile.
You understand pool equipment including single-speed, dual-speed, and variable-speed pumps and their energy efficiency differences.
You know that variable-speed pumps can reduce energy costs by 70-80 percent compared to single-speed pumps.
You are experienced with filter types including sand, cartridge, and DE (diatomaceous earth) filters and their maintenance requirements.
You understand pool chemistry including pH, chlorine, alkalinity, calcium hardness, cyanuric acid, and salt systems.
You know that salt chlorine generators convert salt to chlorine and reduce the need for manual chemical additions.
You are familiar with pool heaters including gas heaters, heat pumps, and solar heating systems.
You understand pool automation systems that control pump speed, lighting, heating, and water features from a phone or panel.
You know that pool resurfacing is needed every 10-15 years for plaster and longer for pebble finishes.
You understand the pool building process including excavation, steel and plumbing, gunite/shotcrete, tile and coping, decking, plaster, and fill/startup.
You are experienced with leak detection methods including pressure testing, dye testing, and electronic leak detection.
You know that weekly service includes testing water chemistry, adding chemicals, skimming, brushing, vacuuming, and checking equipment.
You factor in pool size (gallons) when pricing chemical treatments and service.
You understand local pool barrier and safety codes including fencing, self-closing gates, and drain covers.
You communicate clearly about water chemistry and help homeowners understand the importance of balanced water for surface longevity.

defaultLineItems.description: Dig pool shell to engineered specifications, haul away dirt
Steel rebar cage tied to engineering specs for pool shell reinforcement
Run all suction, return, and equipment plumbing lines
Shoot gunite or shotcrete shell to form pool structure
Decorative tile at waterline and coping stone around pool edge
Interior pool finish - standard white plaster or upgraded pebble finish
Energy-efficient variable-speed pool pump, 1.5-2.0 HP
Pool filter - cartridge, sand, or DE type sized for pool volume
Salt cell system for automatic chlorine generation from salt water
Pool heating system for comfortable water temperature year-round
Color-changing LED pool light with niche
Install all equipment, fill pool, balance chemistry, and commission system
Pool permit, structural engineering, and required inspections
Pump out pool water with proper discharge per local regulations
Chip loose plaster, acid wash, and apply bond coat for adhesion
White marcite plaster finish, hand-applied
Premium pebble finish (PebbleTec, PebbleSheen) instead of standard plaster
Remove and replace waterline tile during resurface
Refill pool, balance water chemistry, and brush daily for first 2 weeks
Replace single-speed with variable-speed pump for energy savings
New pool filter sized for pool volume and pump capacity
Install new salt system or replace aging salt cell
Smart automation panel for pump, lights, heater, and water features
Remove old equipment, plumb and wire new equipment, test and commission

defaultLineItems.itemName: Weekly Pool Cleaning and Chemical Service
defaultLineItems.description: Test water, add chemicals, skim surface, brush walls, vacuum, empty baskets, check equipment
Chlorine, acid, stabilizer, and specialty chemicals as needed
Disassemble, deep clean, and inspect filter cartridges or grids
Restore green or neglected pool to clear, balanced condition
Remove and acid-wash salt chlorinator cell to remove calcium buildup
Pressure test all plumbing lines and perform dye testing at suspected areas
Use electronic listening equipment to pinpoint leaks in shell or plumbing
Excavate and repair leaking underground pipe section
Chip out around crack, apply hydraulic cement and waterproof patch
Repair leak at light niche, return fitting, or skimmer
Perform bucket test to determine if water loss is evaporation or actual leak

terminologyGlossary.gunite: A dry-mix concrete sprayed under high pressure to form the pool shell structure
terminologyGlossary.shotcrete: A wet-mix concrete sprayed to form the pool shell, similar to gunite but different application
terminologyGlossary.plaster: The interior surface finish of a pool, typically white marcite applied over the shell
terminologyGlossary.pebble finish: A premium pool surface with exposed pebbles for durability and aesthetic texture
terminologyGlossary.coping: The cap or edge material around the top of the pool wall where it meets the deck
terminologyGlossary.variable-speed pump: An energy-efficient pump that adjusts RPM to match demand, saving 70-80% on energy
terminologyGlossary.DE filter: Diatomaceous earth filter that provides the finest filtration of pool water
terminologyGlossary.salt chlorine generator: A system that converts dissolved salt into chlorine through electrolysis
terminologyGlossary.TDS: Total dissolved solids - the measure of minerals and other dissolved materials in pool water
terminologyGlossary.calcium hardness: The calcium level in pool water, important for preventing plaster etching or scale buildup
terminologyGlossary.cyanuric acid: A chlorine stabilizer that protects chlorine from UV degradation in outdoor pools
terminologyGlossary.turnover rate: The time it takes for the pump to circulate the entire pool volume once, typically 8-12 hours
terminologyGlossary.skimmer: A built-in device at the waterline that pulls surface water and debris into the filtration system
terminologyGlossary.return jet: Fittings in the pool wall that return filtered, treated water back to the pool
terminologyGlossary.acid wash: Draining the pool and washing the surface with acid to remove stains and restore appearance

seasonalTips.tip: Spring opening season. Market pool startups, equipment inspections, and green-to-clean services. Push salt cell inspections before heavy use.
Pre-summer rush. Book equipment upgrades and minor repairs before homeowners start using pools daily. Variable-speed pump upgrades are a strong sell.
Peak service season. Ensure weekly routes are efficient. Chemical demand is highest in summer heat. Watch for algae outbreaks after storms.
Pool closing and winterization season in cold climates. In warm climates, reduce service frequency and pitch equipment maintenance during the off-peak.

followUpToneNotes: Pool customers value reliability and crystal-clear water. Be knowledgeable about water chemistry without being overly technical. Emphasize the value of consistent weekly service versus sporadic attention. Energy savings from variable-speed pumps are a strong selling point. Discuss surface longevity and how proper chemistry extends the life of plaster and pebble finishes.
